channel.title: KiddieDaily — Animals
channel.description: Wildlife and animal science news for kids.

item.title: Rutinel defeats Bird in Democratic primary for battleground Colorado House seat
item.description: State Rep. Manny Rutinel (D) is projected to defeat former state Rep. Shannon Bird (D) in the Democratic primary to take on Rep. Gabe Evans (R-Colo.), according to Decision Desk HQ. Evans, who represe

item.title: UN chief fears for ‘millions’ of Palestinians amid UNRWA funding shortfall
item.description: Antonio Guterres denounces 'disinformation, smear campaigns' against the UN agency for Palestinian refugees.

item.title: Recent discoveries of ‘lost’ Mekong giant salmon carp renews hope for the fish
item.description: A large fish once feared extinct in Cambodia has been recorded in the country’s waters for the fourth time since 2020, renewing hope for the species. The Mekong giant salmon carp (Aaptosyax grypus), a

item.title: Habitable Worlds Targets in New Star Activity Catalog
item.description: Searching for habitable worlds beyond our solar system consists of more than just having it orbit within its star’s habitable zone, which is the region where temperatures could be just right for liqui

item.title: Myles Smith answers YOUR questions
item.description: British breakthrough star Myles Smith pops into Newsround HQ to answer questions from you!

item.title: Tennis superstar makes a big entrance at Wimbledon
item.description: The Grand Slam winner showed off her latest walk-on outfit for the Wimbledon crowds.

item.title: Elite athletes hope to bring Olympic Games to the North of England
item.description: Huge British sporting stars back Northern Games bid.

item.title: Europe's most active volcano Mount Etna erupts again
item.description: The latest eruption from Mount Etna, which is on the island of Sicily, lit up the night sky and could be seen from miles away. Find out more here.

item.title: ‘I did nothing wrong’: Sweden’s migration regime hardens, upending lives
item.description: Once a European nation receiving large numbers of refugees and migrants, Sweden completes a decade-long overhaul.

item.title: 'A once-in-a-lifetime opportunity' - meet the unlikely England fans looking after the players
item.description: England is one of the four nations that have made Kansas City their home-from-home for the World Cup.

item.title: Pangolins are the world’s most trafficked mammal. A nearly 200-year-old specimen could help.
item.description: Their overlapping scales are believed to be a powerful aphrodisiac. The post Pangolins are the world’s most trafficked mammal. A nearly 200-year-old specimen could help. appeared first on Popula

item.title: Rebel Catholic group SSPX defies pope, consecrates bishops
item.description: An ultra-traditionalist Catholic group has consecrated bishops without papal consent, risking a schism with the church in Rome. Pope Leo had begged the breakaway Society of Saint Pius X to reconsider

item.title: Indonesia’s blackouts reignite debate over coal-dependent energy transition
item.description: JAKARTA — Calls are mounting for Indonesia to accelerate its energy transition after widespread blackouts struck Java and Sumatra in recent weeks, exposing what analysts say are deep vulnerabilities i

item.title: 'Neil The Seal' Blows Up in Size And Popularity, And The Footage Is Everything
item.description: He's up to all sorts of trouble. ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: Chonky Elephant Seal Is Terrorizing an Australian Beach Town, And The Footage Is Everything
item.description: Neil is back and up to all sorts of trouble. ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: Wildlife’s unpredictable movements make climate-change planning difficult
item.description: New England’s winters have long been a punishing force. Forests fade in and out of deep freezes. Animals pile on fat for warmth in anticipation of the harsh weather to come. Others flee south, seeking

item.title: Mosquitoes may learn to like DEET, a common insect repellent
item.description: In lab tests, mosquitoes exposed to DEET while feeding learned to associate the chemical with food.

item.title: 'Neil The Seal' Wreaks Havoc on an Australian City, And The Footage Is Everything
item.description: He's blown up in size and popularity. ScienceAlert stories are written, fact-checked, and edited by humans, never generated by AI. Don't miss a story, subscribe here.

item.title: Largest and Most Detailed Picture Taken of Milky Way’s Center–With Millions of Stars and Thousands of Worlds
item.description: It took a new and special space telescope to parse out each one of these individual stars gleaming in a dizzying mass at the center of our own dear Milky Way galaxy. The European Space Agency’s

item.title: Earth farthest from sun – at aphelion – July 6, 2026
item.description: Earth farthest from sun - aka aphelion - on July 6. That's despite the fact that it's summer in the Northern Hemisphere. The post Earth farthest from sun – at aphelion – July 6, 2026 first appeared on
